subheadline: A calendar humans and agents share
value_proposition: A calendar app with a familiar human UI that also exposes a full REST and WebSocket API so AI agents can own and manage calendars natively.
target_audience: Developer teams and AI builders who want a single calendar layer usable by both humans and autonomous agents in multi-tenant environments.

pricing.price_anchoring: Free to start, upgrade framed as growth-triggered: 'upgrade only when your team grows'

features: Month, week, day views with drag-to-create and dark-mode palette
In-app ZTC agent (Zerg) for natural-language event creation, moving, and resolution
Bidirectional incremental Google Calendar sync via OAuth
RFC 5545 iCal public feeds compatible with Google, Apple, and Outlook
Agent-native REST + WebSocket API with per-agent token provisioning
differentiators: Agent-native WebSocket API — 'your bots can own a calendar too'
'Google-Calendar-simple interface' combined with full programmatic access
Multi-tenant by default with isolated data per organization
Deploy-ready on Fly.io as part of the 'zerg suite'
trust_signals: Live schedule widget implying a real working product

design_patterns.layout: Single-page with full-width hero, a live stats widget floating in the hero, a 3-column features grid, and an accordion FAQ
design_patterns.color_scheme: Dark mode primary palette; no explicit accent colors described in text
design_patterns.typography: Clean, modern sans-serif implied by minimal technical tone
notable_patterns: Live schedule widget in hero showing real-time event counts and agent activity
Feature cards in a 2–3 column grid

key_objections_addressed: Can agents use ZCal directly? — Yes, with API token and WebSocket, no pre-registration required
Does it sync with Google Calendar? — Yes, bidirectional incremental sync via OAuth
Can I subscribe from other apps? — Yes, RFC 5545 iCal feed works with Google, Apple, Outlook
Is there a free tier? — Yes, free to start
missing_or_weak: Zero social proof: no customer logos, testimonials, user counts, or case studies
No pricing page or tier comparison — free vs paid distinction is completely opaque
No screenshots, demo video, or interactive preview of the actual calendar UI
No company credibility signals: no team page, founding story, or 'backed by' mentions
No security or compliance badges despite targeting teams with sensitive scheduling data
Live schedule widget stats ('5 events today', '24 this week') read as demo data, not proof of adoption
No explicit use-case examples or persona-specific messaging beyond 'teams and agents'
standout_elements: Agent-native positioning is genuinely differentiated and clearly articulated throughout
Live schedule widget in hero creates a 'product is real and running' impression
FAQ section directly neutralizes the top four purchase objections
Concise, jargon-appropriate copy for a technical developer audience
Dual-audience framing ('humans and agents') is memorable and novel
